title: Social Media and Mental Health
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Social media (0) has become an integral part of modern life, with billions of people using platforms like Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter daily. However, researchers are increasingly concerned about its impact on mental health, particularly among young people.||Studies have found a correlation (9) between heavy social media use and increased rates of anxiety and depression. One reason for this may/might/could (10) be that people constantly compare themselves to others' carefully curated online personas, (11) which can lead to feelings of inadequacy. The pressure to receive likes and comments is (12) such that a culture has formed in which self-worth is often measured by online validation.||However, social media is not entirely negative. (13) It can help people stay connected with friends and family, especially those who live far away. Online communities provide support for individuals facing similar challenges, and social media (14) has proven effective for raising awareness about important social issues. Experts recommend limiting screen time and being mindful (15) of how social media makes you feel. (16) If you notice it affecting your mood negatively, it may be time to take a break and focus on real-world connections.

example.text: Social media (0) become an integral part of modern life.

questions.explanation: Expression 'correlation between X and Y' indicates a relationship between two factors.
Modal verb with infinitive: 'may/might/could be' expresses possibility.
Relative pronoun referring to the entire previous situation.
Part of the fixed expression 'such that', used to introduce a result or consequence clause.
Subject pronoun referring to social media.
Present perfect: 'has proven' indicates experience up to the present.
Expression 'being mindful of' means being aware or conscious of something.
Conditional conjunction that introduces a possible situation.
